Two of the four flagged binaries are no longer forward: cutaneous sarcoidosis Phase 2 already read out positive, and IMVT-1402 difficult-to-treat RA Period 1 (open-label) read out positive on 2026-05-20 (72.7% ACR20) — only its randomized Period 2 (H2-2026) and the NIU Phase 3 topline (H2-2026) remain ahead. The catalysts clear the strict gate (real/specific/dated/forward, G2), but the stock has already re-rated to its 52-week high on the cumulative positive flow, so the unpriced edge is low; WATCH at 5, not ACTIVE.

verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: Fox Corp/FOXA), G2 (concrete commitment: unanimous-board signed definitive merger agreement w/ controlling-holder Anthony Wood voting agreement), G3 (specific currently-unpriced figure: ROKU $136.53 vs current deal value ~$144.8 [$96 + 0.9693x$50.39 FOXA] = ~$8.3/sh, ~5.7% gross spread to close)

verified.primary_source: Fox Corp / Roku joint press release "Fox Corporation to Acquire Roku, Inc." (prnewswire.com, Jun-15-2026) + ROKU Form 425 on SEC EDGAR (filed Jun-2026); price check FMP batch-quote Jun-2026.
verified.kill_fact: Primary sources confirm everything: Fox-Roku definitive agreement signed Jun-15-2026 (both boards unanimous), $160/sh = $96 cash + 0.9693 FOXA, ~$22B EV, expected 1H-2027 close pending HSR/antitrust + non-US investment-screening + shareholder votes (Anthony Wood + affiliated trusts ~55% voting power signed a voting/support agreement, largely de-risking the vote). Catalyst is forward and UNFIRED: live arb spread persists — ROKU $136.53 vs deal value $96 + 0.9693*$50.39 = $144.84 = ~6.1% gross to close, uncollected because FOXA fell far below the reference price. The one item the line flagged as unverified IS confirmable: the regulatory (reverse) termination fee is $1,237,262,000 (~$1.24B), plus an $866.1M mutual fee, per the merger-agreement summary in the Form 425/8-K. Tradeable as merger-arb long ROKU vs short 0.9693 FOXA, both liquid NASDAQ names. No disqualifier found.
verified.analysis: Fox signed a definitive agreement on Jun-15-2026 to acquire Roku at $160/sh ($96 cash + 0.9693 FOXA), confirmed via Form 425 and both companies' press releases; deal is forward and unclosed with expected H1-2027 close pending HSR/antitrust, non-US regulatory, and shareholder votes (controlling-holder Anthony Wood/LGC Holdco voting agreements largely de-risk the vote). The arb is live: ROKU at $136.53 vs a current deal value of ~$144.8 (FOXA at $50.39, down sharply from the $66.03 reference price) implies a ~5.7% gross spread to close that is not yet collected — so the catalyst is not fired and not fully re-rated. Could not independently confirm the $1.24B reverse break-fee figure (not in the press release; would be in the definitive agreement/S-4), so that specific number is unverified, but the deal structure and milestones are solidly sourced.

verified.catalyst: Topline data from the fully-enrolled Phase 3 upLIFT study of ersodetug in tumor hyperinsulinism (tumor HI) is due in 2H-2026, a separate indication that survived the sunRIZE congenital-HI Phase 3 miss.
verified.dated_milestone: upLIFT tumor-HI Phase 3 topline — expected 2H-2026 (next hard date; company guided "second half of 2026")
verified.bloom_gates: G2 (concrete dated commitment: 2H-2026 topline of a fully-enrolled pivotal study) + G3 (specific unpriced figure: 6 of 8 interim participants already met the ≥50% GIR-reduction primary endpoint, de-risking the readout in a name still depressed from the sunRIZE failure)
verified.edge: M — strong interim (6/8 responders, single-arm open-label so no placebo-confound risk that sank sunRIZE) plus FDA-streamlined ≤16-patient design lowers execution risk; but small N, regulatory path still unsettled, and no public cash-runway confirmation in the verified releases keeps it medium not high.
verified.primary_source: Rezolute press release "Positive Interim Data for its Phase 3 upLIFT Study of Ersodetug in Tumor Hyperinsulinism," GlobeNewswire / ir.rezolutebio.com, 2-Jun-2026
verified.kill_fact: Primary sources confirm every load-bearing claim. Rezolute IR + GlobeNewswire (Jun 2, 2026): upLIFT Phase 3 in tumor HI reported positive interim data — 6 of 8 enrolled patients met the primary endpoint (>=50% reduction in IV glucose-infusion-rate), single-arm open-label ~16-patient design, with fully-enrolled topline guided for 2H-2026 (a genuine FORWARD binary, not fired). This is a distinct indication from the failed sunRIZE congenital-HI Phase 3 (missed primary endpoint Dec 11, 2025; ~45% vs 40% placebo, NS), whose miss was tied to functional-unblinding/caretaker confounds that do not apply to the open-label tumor-HI design — asymmetry holds. The triage's unquantified gap is now CLOSED: per the Q1 2026 10-Q (filed for period ended Mar 31, 2026), RZLT held ~$120.3M cash + marketable securities, materially de-risking financing-overhang/dilution into the readout. Price ~$4.97-$5.10 confirmed; liquid NASDAQ equity, no hidden disqualifier. Only minor nuance vs the line: the magnitude wording is exact and dates accurate, so CONFIRMED rather than corrections.
verified.analysis: Verified: on 2-Jun-2026 Rezolute reported positive interim Phase 3 upLIFT data — 6 of 8 enrolled tumor-HI patients met the primary endpoint (≥50% reduction in IV glucose-infusion-rate) — with topline of the fully-enrolled (≤16-patient, FDA-streamlined single-arm) study guided for 2H-2026. This is a real, dated, forward binary distinct from the failed sunRIZE congenital-HI study, whose miss was attributed to functional-unblinding/caretaker confounds that don't apply to the open-label tumor-HI design, so the asymmetry the triage flagged holds. Gap: I could not confirm cash runway in the verified primary sources, so financing-overhang/dilution risk into the readout is unquantified.

But the high-conviction driver, the SpaceX-proxy equity stake, is already FIRED and re-rated: SpaceX IPO'd June 12, 2026 (~$1.75T), EchoStar's ~2% stake is now mark-to-market, the stock is up 627% YoY and sold off ~11% on the IPO as a "sell-the-news," so the optionality is consensus rather than unpriced. The remaining forward AT&T close is a known, consensus deleveraging event with no edge, and today's WSJ report of an imminent Dish DBS Chapter 11 filing actively contradicts the "going-concern clearing" framing — net WATCH, not ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: SBLK's $470.5M conditional purchase of 16 Diana vessels (1.8M dwt; fleet to 157 ships) only triggers if Diana consummates its hostile takeover of Genco, which remains live — Diana's tender was NOT abandoned at the Jun-26 expiry but extended to Jul-10 and sweetened to $27.34 ($24.80 cash + 1 Diana share).
verified.dated_milestone: Diana/Genco tender offer expiration extended to July 10, 2026, 5:00pm ET (next gate); contingent on definitive merger agreement + poison-pill removal + majority fully-diluted tender (only 28.4% of non-Diana shares tendered as of Jun-26) + SEC F-4 effectiveness.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: Diana Shipping / Genco) + G2 (concrete commitment: signed conditional $470.5M SPA for 16 specified vessels) + G3 (specific unpriced figure: $470.5M / 16 vessels / fleet 157, contingent and unconsummated)
verified.edge: M — real, dated, well-documented second-order leg, but heavily conditioned (needs a still-rejected hostile deal to close: Genco board unanimously rejected $24.80, only 28.4% non-Diana tendered, merger agreement + pill removal + SEC F-4 all still pending); SBLK is the seller's counterparty so upside to SBLK is modest fleet-accretion optionality, not a clean spread.
verified.primary_source: Diana Shipping GlobeNewswire, "Announces Extension of Tender Offer..." (Jun 29, 2026), corroborated by Star Bulk 6-K / GlobeNewswire "Star Bulk to Acquire 16 Vessels... Conditional Upon the Success of Its Offer to Acquire Genco" (Mar 6, 2026)
verified.analysis: The triage premise that the tender "expired Jun-26" is stale/incorrect: Diana extended it to Jul-10 2026 and raised the offer to $27.34, and its board nominees (Ismar, Cornell) won the Jun-18 Genco annual meeting — so the contingent SBLK leg is still alive, not dead. However the deal is far from done: Genco's board has unanimously rejected the offer, only 28.4% of non-Diana shares have tendered (needs a fully-diluted majority), and a definitive merger agreement, poison-pill termination and SEC F-4 effectiveness all remain outstanding. For SBLK specifically this is an indirect, low-conviction fleet-expansion optionality (it is the vessel seller's counterparty, not the arb target), so it warrants WATCH-only — real and dated but contingent on a contested third-party takeover with material completion risk.

This is a genuine forced-seller/asset-sale special situation on a levered underlying — dated, forward, multi-counterparty, with concrete figures — not yet fired and not a macro bet.

verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty Blue Owl) + G2 (binding signed merger agreement, vote approved) both met, but the figures are fully priced — no G3 unpriced figure remains.
verified.primary_source: StockTitan / Sila press release "Stockholders Approve Acquisition by Affiliates of Blue Owl Capital" (vote 2026-06-26, close expected 2026-07-01); FMP quote 2026-06-29 $30.36.
verified.analysis: The catalyst is real and dated (named counterparty Blue Owl, signed all-cash $30.38/sh merger, shareholder vote passed 98% on June 26 2026) but it has fully fired: the 19% premium re-rated into the price on April 20, and SILA now trades $30.36 vs the $30.38 deal price — a ~$0.02 / ~0.07% gross spread with close expected ~July 1. No unpriced (G3) figure or forward return remains; this is a closing-week stub, not an actionable spread. Tier NONE: the milestone is real but the edge is gone.

verified.catalyst: Two summer-2026 cystic-fibrosis clinical readouts — SION-719 PreciSION CF Phase 2a proof-of-concept (Trikafta add-on, F508del-homozygous) and the SION-451 dual-combination Phase 1 — the first clinical validation of an NBD1-stabilizer mechanism.
verified.dated_milestone: SION-719 Phase 2a topline + SION-451 dual-combo Phase 1 topline, both summer 2026 (next: enrollment completed Apr 27, 2026; readouts guided summer 2026)
verified.instrument: equity (SION, NASDAQ); options available for binary expression
verified.bloom_gates: G3 — specific currently-unpriced figure: dated proof-of-concept efficacy readout for a novel NBD1-stabilizer mechanism (first-in-disease), summer 2026; clinical binary not yet resolved
verified.edge: M — genuine dated dual binary on a differentiated mechanism with ~$290M cash (runway to 2028, no financing overhang), but stock trades near 52-wk highs (~3x year-low, +14% vs 200-day) so meaningful optionality is already priced, and a Trikafta add-on POC in well-controlled patients is a high-bar/high-variance coin-flip.
verified.primary_source: Sionna Q1 2026 financial results, May 12, 2026 (cash $289.9M to 2028; both summer-2026 readouts reaffirmed) + enrollment-complete PR Apr 27, 2026 (investors.sionnatx.com / GlobeNewswire); live quote FMP 2026-06-29
verified.kill_fact: PreciSION CF Phase 2a (SION-719) enrollment completed Apr 27, 2026 (GlobeNewswire/SEC 8-K) with topline reaffirmed for summer 2026 in the May 12, 2026 Q1 release; SION-451 dual-combo Phase 1 topline also guided summer 2026 — both forward and unfired as of 2026-06-29. Cash $289.9M at Mar 31, 2026 funds ops into 2028 (no near-term dilution gate). Mechanism: first clinical NBD1-stabilizer add-on to Trikafta. Live FMP quote $42.97 on NASDAQ, vs 52-wk low $15.10 / high $48.445 — already re-rated hard, so catalyst partly anticipated and binary is high-variance; ACTIVE-6 warranted, not higher.
verified.analysis: Sionna's two summer-2026 readouts are real, dated, forward and unfired: PreciSION CF Phase 2a (SION-719) enrollment completed Apr 27, 2026 with topline reaffirmed for summer 2026 in the May 12, 2026 Q1 release, and the SION-451 dual-combination Phase 1 topline is also guided to summer 2026. The thesis rests on the first clinical demonstration that an NBD1 stabilizer adds benefit on top of Trikafta — a genuinely novel mechanism (G3 unpriced figure), funded into 2028 by $289.9M cash so there is no near-term dilution gate. Caveat: at $42.97 (vs $15.10 52-wk low) the name has already re-rated hard, so the catalyst is partly anticipated and the binary is high-variance; an ACTIVE-6 is warranted but not a higher conviction.

verified.catalyst: SiTime is the acquirer of Renesas' timing business (~$1.5B cash + 4.13M shares), expected to close Q3 CY2026 — an integration/execution bet, not a capturable take-out spread.
verified.dated_milestone: Deal close expected Q3 CY2026 (by end-2026); HSR waiting period already expired May 8, 2026; $1.2B converts priced + $900M Wells Fargo bridge committed.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty Renesas) and G2 (signed APA + secured financing) pass; G3 fails — no specific currently-unpriced figure (deal/financing already re-rated, stock near 52-wk high).
verified.edge: L — acquirer-side, so no merger-arb spread exists to capture; the deal, $1.2B convert financing, and HSR clearance are all done and digested by the market (SITM +5.7% to $710, 200-day avg $422, near 52-wk high $901). Only forward item is customary closing.
verified.primary_source: SiTime Form 8-K / press release "SiTime to Acquire Renesas' Timing Business" (Feb 4, 2026) and convertible notes pricing release (sitime.com), confirmed via SEC EDGAR FY2026 8-K filings.
verified.analysis: SITM signed an Asset Purchase Agreement Feb 4, 2026 to buy Renesas' timing business for ~$1.5B cash + 4,130,644 shares; HSR expired May 8, 2026, the $1.2B convertible notes are priced, and a $900M Wells Fargo bridge is committed, so the transformative catalyst has effectively fired and been heavily re-rated. Because SITM is the acquirer there is no take-out spread to harvest — the only remaining event is a customary Q3-2026 close, making this an integration/synergy bet rather than a dated, currently-unpriced forward catalyst. Fails the strict merger_arb gate (not forward-unpriced, already fired/re-rated): tier NONE.

verified.catalyst: IonQ's pending acquisition of SkyWater at $35.00/share ($15 cash + $20 IonQ stock, collared) is set to close Q2/Q3 2026 pending HSR/ITAR (likely CFIUS) clearance.
verified.dated_milestone: Regulatory close expected Q2/Q3 2026 (no public drop-dead date confirmed); shareholders already approved May 8, 2026.
verified.instrument: SKYT common (merger consideration $15 cash + $20 IONQ stock, collar 0.3326–0.5265 IONQ sh)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty IonQ) and G2 (definitive, shareholder-approved merger agreement) pass; G3 FAILS — no currently-unpriced figure remains.
verified.edge: L — the arb spread has collapsed to NEGATIVE: SKYT at $35.60 vs deal value ~$35.00 (IONQ $53.88 inside the $37.99–$60.13 collar pays the full $20 stock leg). Market prices SKYT ~1.7% ABOVE terms, so the catalyst is effectively fired/priced; residual is just IONQ stock-leg beta, not a clean spread.
verified.primary_source: SkyWater Form 8-K / press release, shareholder approval May 8, 2026 (businesswire 2026-05-08); deal terms SkyWater Form 425/8-K Jan 2026; live quotes SKYT $35.60 / IONQ $53.88 (FMP, 2026-06-29).
verified.analysis: The merger is real, specific, dated and forward (close pending in Q2/Q3 2026 with regulatory clearances outstanding), satisfying G1/G2, but the actionable arb edge is gone: SKYT at $35.60 trades at a ~-1.7% spread to the ~$35.00 deal value with IONQ inside its collar, so there is no positive unpriced spread to harvest (G3 fails). What remains is exposure to IONQ stock-leg beta — a directional bet on a volatile quantum name (IONQ +9.3% today), not a discrete unpriced merger catalyst. GAP: I could not find a public outside/termination date or a specific CFIUS-status update; close is broadly guided to Q2/Q3 2026 only.

verified.catalyst: Texas Instruments' definitive all-cash acquisition of Silicon Labs at $231.00/sh (~$7.5B) awaits foreign antitrust clearance (notably China SAMR) before closing.
verified.dated_milestone: Foreign/China SAMR clearance pending; expected close H1 2027; merger outside date Feb 4 2027, extendable to Aug 4 2027 and Feb 4 2028
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty Texas Instruments / Caldwell Merger Corp) + G2 (definitive merger agreement, $231.00 cash, $499M reverse termination fee, signed Feb 4 2026, shareholder-approved)
verified.edge: L — gross spread only $11.97 ($219.03 vs $231.00) ≈ 5.5% absolute over ~9-15 mo to a 2027-2028 outside date ≈ 6-7% annualized; that is fair compensation for the residual China/foreign-clearance risk, fully priced, not an exploitable mispricing.
verified.primary_source: SLAB DEFM14A / 8-K filings via StockTitan + MLex "merger filing not yet accepted in China" (mid-June 2026); FMP quote $219.03 (2026-06-29)
verified.analysis: Real, named, dated definitive all-cash merger: TI to acquire SLAB at $231.00/sh, signed Feb 4 2026, shareholders approved, US HSR cleared (expired May 22 2026). But the triage's "closing-pending" framing is wrong — closing is targeted H1 2027 with the outside date Feb 4 2027 extendable to Feb 4 2028, and China SAMR had not even accepted the filing as of mid-June 2026, so the only forward gate is multi-quarter foreign antitrust. The terms are fully public and the ~5.5% gross / ~6-7% annualized spread is the market's priced risk premium, not unpriced edge — fails the strict forward/unpriced gate; WATCH for a re-rate only on a concrete China-clearance dated trigger.
